mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects rising labor income and consumer spending capacity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises the equity discount rate and slows credit-sensitive spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Federal funds rate at 4.99% as of June 2006
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in retail sales (RSAFS) and increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) identify a joint demand-labor contraction that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, positive month-over-month changes in industrial production (INDPRO) capture coincident corporate revenue momentum and predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of months when INDPRO is rising during identified late-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) below 2000 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment that precedes broader demand weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months when HOUST is below 2000.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month changes in industrial production (INDPRO) reflect real-time supply-side economic momentum and predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO changes and 5-day returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a rolling 3-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate revenue forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Falling housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) identify a joint housing-labor market deterioration that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances over any 3-year window.
